ocr.text: In the analysis of accelerated scan pairs, the WMH volume (mm3) for subjects with both (if available) 0–6 and 0–12 month
scan pairs that passed QC is 6.55 (8.96), n = 498, while for those where one or both failed QC, it is 9.40 (9.71), n = 66, resulting
in an adjusted difference of 2.01a[0.01, 4.62],p ≤ 0.05. The average age (years) for those passing QC is 72.2 (7.4), n = 513,
compared to 74.3 (6.7), n = 66 for those failing QC, showing an adjusted difference of 2.2b[0.4, 4.0],p = 0.02.
Both (if available) 0–6 and 0–12 month scan pairs passed QC  One or both of the 0–6 and 0–12 month scan pairs failed QC  Adjusted difference [95% CI]
Accelerated scan pairs
% Subjects cognitively impaired  73%  78%  4% [−6, 15], p = 0.45
Non-accelerated scan pairs
% Subjects cognitively impaired  71%  82%  11% [19, 3],p = 0.01
The baseline MMSE/30 is 27.8 (2.4), n = 513 for those passing QC and 27.7 (2.1), n = 66 for those failing, with an adjusted
difference of 0.1c [−0.3, 0.4], p > 0.05. The percentage of subjects cognitively impaired is 73% for those passing QC and 78%
for those failing, indicating a 4% [−6, 15], p = 0.45 difference. For non-accelerated scan pairs, the WMH volume (mm3) is 6.85
(9.28), n = 444 for those passing QC and 7.01 (8.40), n = 120 for those failing, yielding an adjusted difference of -0.32a [−1.82,
1.49], p > 0.05. The age (years) for passing QC is 72.3 (7.2), n = 455, versus 72.9 (7.6), n = 124 for failing, producing an
adjusted difference of 0.9b [−0.6, 2.3], p = 0.24. The baseline MMSE/30 is 27.9 (2.3), n = 455 for those passing QC and 27.3
(2.5), n = 124 for those failing, showing an adjusted difference of -0.3c[−0.6, −0.1],p ≤ 0.05. Finally, the percentage of subjects
